page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server within the High Performance family. It is configured with 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. The hardware profile includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a combination of average and limited performance characteristics, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. Single-core processing is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1450.08 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 8.95 GB/sec, both placing in the middle 50% of tested instances. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show clear tradeoffs. The stress-ng multi-core score of 5759.17 and the PassMark CPU score of 6501.58 fall into the bottom 25% of benchmarks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is further constrained, registering in the bottom 10% at 17.62 GB/sec. While cached small-block memory reads perform at an average rate of 416.34 GB/sec, large-block uncached memory reads are limited to 33.43 GB/sec.

This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial local storage relative to memory, due to its 180 GB NVMe SSD capacity. However, the shared CPU model makes it less suitable for sustained high-compute tasks, heavy database operations, or large-scale LLM inference. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, Redis caching—where it achieves 6.06 million SET operations per second—development environments, and basic file processing.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show average single-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1450.08 and 6.06 million Redis SET operations per second. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show performance trade-offs, with multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the server a cost-effective choice for workloads requiring substantial local storage but moderate CPU utilization. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments rather than sustained high-compute or database-heavy applications.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 180 GB of NVMe SSD storage, suitable for lightweight workloads and caching.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is an x86_64 server with 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared quad-core Intel processing with generous local NVMe storage for lightweight applications and caching.
bullet_points: 4 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory paired with 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core performance and high-speed Redis caching capabilities
Shared CPU allocation presenting multi-core performance tradeoffs
Well-suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments
